patterns.name: Wolf Style
patterns.summary: A very short pattern with early friction and outside scoring opportunities.
patterns.play_strategy: Keep the breakpoint outside and manage early hook. Straighter angles often beat big swing early.
patterns.ball_motion: Early read and strong friction response.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Play very outside, often near 3-6 at the arrows with a breakpoint close to the gutter.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Play very outside on the left side, often near 34-37 at the arrows with a breakpoint close to the gutter.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Urethane or smooth low-flare reactive to control overreaction.
patterns.common_adjustments: If the ball hooks too early, move inside but keep the breakpoint outside. If it misses the headpin, square up or add surface.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Early dry boards create fast response to friction.
Middle oil encourages controlled launch angles.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: 1-4 board, extremely outside
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Forward to moderate rotation; keep the ball from standing up too early.
play_profile.friction_response: Immediate friction response and early hook. Overreaction is the main threat.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Small. Tugged shots can hang in oil or jump when they find friction at the wrong angle.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Moderate only if the ball stays outside. Missing the breakpoint right can still hook early.
play_profile.hold_area: Hold is very limited because the pattern is short and friction appears early.
play_profile.recovery_area: Gutter-adjacent boards are the scoring recovery zone.
play_profile.speed_control: Do not get slow early. Speed helps keep the ball online through the heads.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: High rev players usually need urethane or very smooth weak covers. Lower rev players can use controlled reactive.
play_profile.spare_priority: High priority. Outside friction makes corner-pin spare shooting sensitive.

transitions.what_to_watch: Ball reads immediately outside and can over-hook early.
transitions.move_strategy: Play very direct to an outside breakpoint.
transitions.ball_change: Urethane or weak smooth reactive.

transitions.what_to_watch: Shots hook at the arrows or leave weak corners from early roll.
transitions.move_strategy: Move in, keep breakpoint outside, and add speed if needed.
transitions.ball_change: Cleaner/weaker cover when urethane burns up.

transitions.what_to_watch: Inside friction lets some players create more angle.
transitions.move_strategy: Open only enough to keep the ball from reading too soon.
transitions.ball_change: Controlled reactive can replace urethane when carry disappears.

equipment_options.when_to_use: Use to control early friction and keep the ball close to the pocket.
Use if urethane does not create enough entry angle.
Use after the fronts burn and urethane starts leaving weak corners.

lane_intelligence.oil_shape: Very short pattern with immediate outside friction and early hook.
lane_intelligence.friction_expectation: Friction appears quickly in the front and outside part of the lane.
lane_intelligence.scoring_pace: Medium to high scoring if early hook is controlled.
lane_intelligence.breakpoint_window: Breakpoint must stay extremely outside early; shape opens only after transition.
lane_intelligence.miss_risk: Main risk is hooking at the arrows or leaving weak corners from roll-out.
lane_intelligence.first_move_trigger: Move when the ball stands up early or the same good shot starts crossing high.
lane_intelligence.surface_guidance: Urethane or weak smooth reactive at 1000-2000 grit; cleaner covers after burn.
lane_intelligence.practice_focus: Test launch speed and hand position so the ball does not read before the arrows.

external_refs.reference_note: BOWL.com lists Wolf as a 32-foot PBA Experience animal pattern and links the official Kegel PDF with 27.55 mL total oil.
Kegel is the official reference source for pattern graph, load, PDF, and KOSI data. StrikeIQ uses this entry for strategy and visualization context.
PBA.com lists Wolf as a current Animal Patterns oil pattern download. Current PBA versions may differ from legacy BOWL.com/Kegel PDF versions.

patterns.name: Cheetah Style
patterns.summary: A short pattern that encourages outside angles and early friction.
patterns.play_strategy: Keep the breakpoint closer to the gutter and use the early dry boards to shape the shot. Avoid sending the ball too far inside early.
patterns.ball_motion: Earlier hook with strong response to friction.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Play outside, often around 5-8 at the arrows with a breakpoint near 3-5.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Play outside from the left side, often around 32-35 at the arrows with a breakpoint near 36-38.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Urethane, smooth reactive, or lower-flare equipment can control the early friction.
patterns.common_adjustments: If the ball jumps, move left and keep the breakpoint outside. If it misses right, square up or use more surface.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Early dry boards create fast response to friction.
Middle oil encourages controlled launch angles.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: 3-6 board, close to the gutter
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Moderate rotation with controlled tilt; too much side roll can make the ball jump sideways.
play_profile.friction_response: Very fast response to outside friction with early hook risk.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Small. Inside misses can hang if they never touch friction or jump if they hit dry early.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Moderate if breakpoint stays outside; missed projection too far right can still over-read.
play_profile.hold_area: Hold is inside the oil line, but it is not wide. Keep launch angles disciplined.
play_profile.recovery_area: Primary recovery is the outside 1-5 board area.
play_profile.speed_control: Avoid slow, grabby shots. Speed down only when the ball will not pick up.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: High rev players should control flare and surface. Lower rev players can use smoother reactive to avoid weak corners.
play_profile.spare_priority: Corner pins matter. Flatten the hand for 10/7 pins because the outside friction is strong.

transitions.what_to_watch: Ball reads very early when it touches outside friction.
transitions.move_strategy: Keep launch direct and breakpoint near the gutter.
transitions.ball_change: Urethane or smooth reactive controls the first read.

transitions.what_to_watch: Ball hooks too early or leaves weak corners after burning energy.
transitions.move_strategy: Move in with the feet while keeping breakpoint outside.
transitions.ball_change: Go cleaner or weaker if the ball burns up.

transitions.what_to_watch: Players can open the lane, but over/under appears quickly.
transitions.move_strategy: Project through the track without sending it past the gutter friction.
transitions.ball_change: Use cleaner angular reactive only when carry requires it.

equipment_options.when_to_use: Use when outside friction is strong and early hook must be controlled.
Use if urethane leaves weak corners or cannot continue.
Use only after the lane opens enough to need more continuation.

lane_intelligence.oil_shape: Short outside pattern that creates early friction near the gutter.
lane_intelligence.friction_expectation: Fast response outside; balls with too much backend can stand up early.
lane_intelligence.scoring_pace: High scoring for players who control launch and keep the breakpoint outside.
lane_intelligence.breakpoint_window: Breakpoint must stay close to the gutter early; moving it too far inside creates hang.
lane_intelligence.miss_risk: Main risk is early hook and weak corners from burning energy too soon.
lane_intelligence.first_move_trigger: Move when the ball hooks before the breakpoint or drives high from good shots.
lane_intelligence.surface_guidance: Urethane or smooth low-flare reactive at 1000-3000 grit; cleaner only after burn.
lane_intelligence.practice_focus: Confirm how far right the breakpoint can go before overreaction appears.

external_refs.reference_note: BOWL.com lists Cheetah as a 33-foot PBA Experience animal pattern and links the official Kegel PDF with 27.05 mL total oil.

patterns.name: Viper Style
patterns.summary: An official BOWL.com PBA Experience animal pattern with verified PDF length and volume.
patterns.play_strategy: Keep launch angles direct and use the outside friction without letting the ball hook too early.
patterns.ball_motion: Earlier read with faster response to friction.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Start outside, often around 5-9 at the arrows, with a breakpoint near the gutter-side friction.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Start outside from the left side, often around 31-35 at the arrows, with a breakpoint near the gutter-side friction.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Urethane or smooth low-flare reactive to control early friction.
patterns.common_adjustments: Make small moves, watch repeated leaves, and change surface or ball shape only after the same reaction appears on quality shots.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Outside zone; short patterns expose this area early, while longer patterns reduce free recovery.
Track and middle blend; match ball speed, surface, and launch angle to this zone.
Mirror-side oil shape for left-side read and cross-lane visual balance.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: Rule-of-31 estimate near board 5, then refine from practice shots.
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Forward to moderate rotation for early friction control.
play_profile.friction_response: Fast friction response outside; overreaction is the first risk.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Limited to moderate. Treat inside misses as information, not automatic hold.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Useful only if the breakpoint stays outside and speed is stable.
play_profile.hold_area: Small hold area around the selected target window.
play_profile.recovery_area: Recovery is closest to the gutter-side friction.
play_profile.speed_control: Repeat speed first; speed misses change breakpoint faster than target moves.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: Higher rev players should smooth response. Lower rev players may need stronger surface to create shape.
play_profile.spare_priority: High priority. Sport conditions reward clean frames and simple spare leaves.

transitions.what_to_watch: Confirm whether the ball reads early, blends, or skids past the breakpoint.
transitions.move_strategy: Start with the official length-based target window and make small moves from repeated shots.
transitions.ball_change: Urethane or smooth low-flare reactive.

transitions.what_to_watch: Watch for repeated high hits, weak corners, buckets, or washouts from the same good shot.
transitions.move_strategy: Move with the friction while preserving the breakpoint window.
transitions.ball_change: Change surface or cover only when the current shape no longer controls the pocket.

transitions.what_to_watch: Front-lane friction and carrydown may create over/under.
transitions.move_strategy: Choose the line that leaves the easiest spares and protects the pocket.
transitions.ball_change: Cleaner cover if fronts are burned; more surface if the ball skids through the spot.

equipment_options.when_to_use: Use for the first look at the official pattern before chasing transition.
Use when backend motion is too sharp or pocket control is fragile.
Use when the fronts are gone and the current ball loses continuation.

lane_intelligence.oil_shape: Short sport pattern with early outside friction.
lane_intelligence.friction_expectation: Friction appears early and can make the ball stand up before the breakpoint.
lane_intelligence.scoring_pace: Can score when early hook is controlled.
lane_intelligence.target_window_right: Feet 6-12, eyes 4-9, breakpoint near the outside friction.
lane_intelligence.target_window_left: Feet 28-34, eyes 31-36, breakpoint near the outside friction.
lane_intelligence.breakpoint_window: Start near board 5 from the rule of 31, then refine from practice.
lane_intelligence.miss_risk: Main risk is early hook and weak corners from roll-out.
lane_intelligence.first_move_trigger: Move after repeated good shots show the same leave or breakpoint change.
lane_intelligence.surface_guidance: Control cover and surface first; cleaner pieces come later.
lane_intelligence.practice_focus: Use practice to confirm breakpoint board, spare reaction, and the first move direction.

patterns.name: Johnny Petraglia Style
patterns.summary: An official BOWL.com PBA Experience hall of fame pattern with verified PDF length and volume.
patterns.play_strategy: Find the best blended target window during practice and keep the ball motion smooth through the breakpoint.
patterns.ball_motion: Balanced midlane read with controlled downlane motion.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Start near 11-16 at the arrows and adjust based on the strongest blend of hold and recovery.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Start near 24-29 at the arrows and adjust based on the strongest blend of hold and recovery.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Benchmark reactive or controlled solid with surface matched to lane friction.
patterns.common_adjustments: Make small moves, watch repeated leaves, and change surface or ball shape only after the same reaction appears on quality shots.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Outside zone; short patterns expose this area early, while longer patterns reduce free recovery.
Track and middle blend; match ball speed, surface, and launch angle to this zone.
Mirror-side oil shape for left-side read and cross-lane visual balance.

play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Moderate to forward roll to control midlane read and entry angle.
play_profile.friction_response: Balanced response with a narrow but playable blend.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Limited to moderate. Treat inside misses as information, not automatic hold.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Limited. Outside misses can skate if they cross the oil edge too late.
play_profile.hold_area: Small hold area around the selected target window.
play_profile.recovery_area: Recovery is closer to the track than the gutter.
play_profile.speed_control: Repeat speed first; speed misses change breakpoint faster than target moves.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: Higher rev players should smooth response. Lower rev players may need stronger surface to create shape.
play_profile.spare_priority: High priority. Sport conditions reward clean frames and simple spare leaves.

transitions.what_to_watch: Watch for repeated high hits, weak corners, buckets, or washouts from the same good shot.

patterns.name: Mike Aulby Style
patterns.summary: An official BOWL.com PBA Experience hall of fame pattern with verified PDF length and volume.
patterns.play_strategy: Find the best blended target window during practice and keep the ball motion smooth through the breakpoint.
patterns.ball_motion: Balanced midlane read with controlled downlane motion.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Start near 11-16 at the arrows and adjust based on the strongest blend of hold and recovery.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Start near 24-29 at the arrows and adjust based on the strongest blend of hold and recovery.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Benchmark reactive or controlled solid with surface matched to lane friction.
patterns.common_adjustments: Make small moves, watch repeated leaves, and change surface or ball shape only after the same reaction appears on quality shots.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Outside zone; short patterns expose this area early, while longer patterns reduce free recovery.
Track and middle blend; match ball speed, surface, and launch angle to this zone.
Mirror-side oil shape for left-side read and cross-lane visual balance.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: Rule-of-31 estimate near board 7, then refine from practice shots.
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Moderate to forward roll to control midlane read and entry angle.
play_profile.friction_response: Balanced response with a narrow but playable blend.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Limited to moderate. Treat inside misses as information, not automatic hold.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Limited. Outside misses can skate if they cross the oil edge too late.
play_profile.hold_area: Small hold area around the selected target window.
play_profile.recovery_area: Recovery is closer to the track than the gutter.
play_profile.speed_control: Repeat speed first; speed misses change breakpoint faster than target moves.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: Higher rev players should smooth response. Lower rev players may need stronger surface to create shape.
play_profile.spare_priority: High priority. Sport conditions reward clean frames and simple spare leaves.

transitions.what_to_watch: Confirm whether the ball reads early, blends, or skids past the breakpoint.
transitions.move_strategy: Start with the official length-based target window and make small moves from repeated shots.
transitions.ball_change: Benchmark reactive or controlled solid.

transitions.what_to_watch: Watch for repeated high hits, weak corners, buckets, or washouts from the same good shot.
transitions.move_strategy: Move with the friction while preserving the breakpoint window.
transitions.ball_change: Change surface or cover only when the current shape no longer controls the pocket.

transitions.what_to_watch: Front-lane friction and carrydown may create over/under.
transitions.move_strategy: Choose the line that leaves the easiest spares and protects the pocket.
transitions.ball_change: Cleaner cover if fronts are burned; more surface if the ball skids through the spot.

equipment_options.when_to_use: Use for the first look at the official pattern before chasing transition.
Use when backend motion is too sharp or pocket control is fragile.
Use when the fronts are gone and the current ball loses continuation.

lane_intelligence.oil_shape: Medium sport pattern with a blended target window.
lane_intelligence.friction_expectation: Friction is present but narrow; repeat launch angle and speed.
lane_intelligence.scoring_pace: Medium scoring with accurate target control.
lane_intelligence.target_window_right: Feet 13-18, eyes 10-16, breakpoint near the rule-of-31 board.
lane_intelligence.target_window_left: Feet 22-27, eyes 24-30, breakpoint near the mirrored rule-of-31 board.
lane_intelligence.breakpoint_window: Start near board 7 from the rule of 31, then refine from practice.
lane_intelligence.miss_risk: Main risk is over-adjusting before the best blend is confirmed.
lane_intelligence.first_move_trigger: Move after repeated good shots show the same leave or breakpoint change.
lane_intelligence.surface_guidance: Start benchmark, then tune surface for backend response.
lane_intelligence.practice_focus: Use practice to confirm breakpoint board, spare reaction, and the first move direction.

patterns.name: Chameleon Style
patterns.summary: A medium pattern that can support more than one playable angle.
patterns.play_strategy: Read the lane during practice and choose the best blend of hold and friction. Both direct and moderate inside lines can work.
patterns.ball_motion: Balanced shape with enough length to choose different launch angles.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Start near 12-15 at the arrows and adjust based on friction and carry.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Start near 25-28 at the arrows and adjust based on friction and carry.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Benchmark reactive is a good starting point; adjust surface for lane friction.
patterns.common_adjustments: Move in as the track opens. If the backend is too sharp, use a smoother cover or more forward roll.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Blend area can support several angles depending on transition.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: 7-10 board, can migrate with transition
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Versatile. Moderate rotation works, with forward roll when the backend gets sharp.
play_profile.friction_response: Balanced friction response; multiple lines can score if the ball shape is matched.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Moderate. Inside misses hold better than short sport patterns, but not like house.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Moderate. Outside recovery exists when the breakpoint is not too deep.
play_profile.hold_area: Middle blend lets direct and mild swing players both find pocket control.
play_profile.recovery_area: Track friction between 7-10 is usually the first useful recovery area.
play_profile.speed_control: Use speed to shape. Faster players may need surface; slower players may need cleaner shells.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: Matches many rev rates. Adjust launch angle before making large ball changes.
play_profile.spare_priority: Medium-high priority. It can play scorable, but transition creates washout and bucket leaves.

transitions.what_to_watch: Several lines may look usable; carry tells which is real.
transitions.move_strategy: Compare direct and moderate swing, then commit.
transitions.ball_change: Benchmark reactive first.

transitions.what_to_watch: One zone starts giving better carry and miss room.
transitions.move_strategy: Follow the best friction pair with controlled moves.
transitions.ball_change: Surface change before drastic ball change.

transitions.what_to_watch: Direct players lose hold or swing players lose recovery.
transitions.move_strategy: Either move in and shape more, or square up with smoother motion.
transitions.ball_change: Cleaner pearl/hybrid for more angle; solid for control.

equipment_options.when_to_use: Use to identify whether direct or mild swing has better carry.
Use when backend is too fast or the pocket is unstable.
Use when fronts go away and you need more downlane recovery.

lane_intelligence.oil_shape: Blended medium pattern that can support more than one angle.
lane_intelligence.friction_expectation: Friction response is readable but not automatic; carry decides the best zone.
lane_intelligence.scoring_pace: Medium to high scoring once the best line is identified.
lane_intelligence.breakpoint_window: Breakpoint can migrate with transition; direct and mild swing may both work.
lane_intelligence.miss_risk: Main risk is committing to a line that hits pocket but carries poorly.
lane_intelligence.first_move_trigger: Move when one side of the pocket starts leaving repeated weak corners or buckets.
lane_intelligence.surface_guidance: Benchmark 3000 grit first; adjust surface before making a drastic ball change.
lane_intelligence.practice_focus: Compare a direct line and a mild swing line, then choose the one with better carry.

patterns.name: Typical House Shot
patterns.summary: A forgiving league pattern with more oil in the middle and drier outside boards.
patterns.play_strategy: Use the dry outside area as recovery and keep the ball near the track area. Misses toward the gutter can recover, while misses inside tend to hold.
patterns.ball_motion: Predictable skid through the front with a readable move off the dry boards.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Start around 15 with the feet and project toward 8-10 at the arrows or breakpoint.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Start around 25 with the feet and project toward 30-32 at the arrows or breakpoint.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Medium reactive ball for most bowlers; stronger cover if the lane has heavier volume.
patterns.common_adjustments: Move inside as the track dries. If the ball over-hooks, add speed, move in, or switch to a cleaner cover.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Outside boards have less oil and provide recovery on missed shots.
Track area has blend between hold and recovery.
Middle boards hold the ball and protect tugged shots.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: 7-11 board at the breakpoint
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Moderate axis rotation, enough side roll to use friction without over-skidding.
play_profile.friction_response: Strong recovery outside with hold in the middle; the lane helps blend common misses.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Good. Shots leaked two to four boards inside can hold pocket if speed is stable.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Good. Outside misses often recover unless the cover is too clean or speed is too high.
play_profile.hold_area: Middle oil from roughly 15-25 keeps tugged shots from crossing early.
play_profile.recovery_area: Dry boards outside 7-8 are the main recovery zone.
play_profile.speed_control: Keep speed repeatable. Extra speed can create weak corners; slow speed can make the dry jump.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: Lower rev players can use stronger cover. Higher rev players usually need cleaner shape or more inside room.
play_profile.spare_priority: Normal spare discipline, but avoid hooking at corner pins once the outside track is burned.

transitions.what_to_watch: Ball reads the dry and drives through the pins with miss room both ways.
transitions.move_strategy: Start near the track and confirm the outside recovery board.
transitions.ball_change: Benchmark reactive is usually enough.

transitions.what_to_watch: High flush hits, flat 10s, or shots crossing high as the outside dries.
transitions.move_strategy: Move feet and eyes inside together, usually 2-and-1 or 3-and-2.
transitions.ball_change: Stay with the same ball if carry holds; go cleaner if it reads too soon.

transitions.what_to_watch: Ball either jumps off dry or skids past carrydown downlane.
transitions.move_strategy: Blend the cliff by moving in and reducing launch angle.
transitions.ball_change: Cleaner cover for early hook, stronger surface for skid/weak corners.

equipment_options.when_to_use: Best first look when the lane has normal league volume.
Use when the ball misses the breakpoint or leaves weak 10s/7s.
Use when the outside friction makes the ball jump too early.

lane_intelligence.oil_shape: Crowned house shape with heavier middle oil and dry outside boards.
lane_intelligence.friction_expectation: Outside friction should be available early; middle boards should hold most common tug misses.
lane_intelligence.scoring_pace: High scoring if spare shooting stays clean and the ball clears the fronts.
lane_intelligence.target_window_right: Feet 14-18, eyes 8-11, breakpoint 7-10 as a first look.
lane_intelligence.target_window_left: Feet 22-26, eyes 29-32, breakpoint 31-34 as a first look.
lane_intelligence.breakpoint_window: Use the dry outside edge first, then migrate in as the track burns.
lane_intelligence.miss_risk: Main risk is over/under after transition: jump off dry or skid through carrydown.
lane_intelligence.first_move_trigger: Move when flush hits become high, flat corners appear, or the ball reads before the arrows.
lane_intelligence.surface_guidance: Start 3000-4000 grit or lane shine; add surface only when volume beats the cover.
lane_intelligence.practice_focus: Confirm outside recovery and inside hold with one miss-room test each direction.

patterns.name: Custom Practice Template
patterns.summary: A flexible template pattern for bowlers or centers to model a local lane condition before official graph data is imported.
patterns.play_strategy: Use this as a baseline for entering local observations: where the ball skids, where it reads, and which boards create hold or recovery.
patterns.ball_motion: Neutral reaction shape intended to be adjusted from bowler notes and observed transition.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Start near 12-15 at the arrows and update the line from practice shots.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Start near 26-29 at the arrows and update the line from practice shots.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Benchmark reactive first, then adjust by lane friction and user notes.
patterns.common_adjustments: Update the zones, target boards, and ball notes as the center condition is learned.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Baseline outside friction area for user observation.
Baseline track and middle blend for local practice notes.
Mirrored outside zone for left-handed reads and custom edits.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: 8-11 board, update from practice shots
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Use a neutral release first, then record how axis rotation changes breakpoint.
play_profile.friction_response: User-defined response based on the local center condition and notes.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Unknown until observed. Use the first game to mark whether tugged shots hold.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Unknown until observed. Track whether pushed shots recover or hang.
play_profile.hold_area: Baseline middle zone can be edited from user observation.
play_profile.recovery_area: Baseline outside zone can be edited from user observation.
play_profile.speed_control: Record speed misses and whether the ball reads early or skids past the spot.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: Use notes to compare lower and higher rev reactions on the same condition.
play_profile.spare_priority: Normal spare discipline while the custom pattern is being mapped.

patterns.name: Don Johnson Style
patterns.summary: An official BOWL.com PBA Experience hall of fame pattern with verified PDF length and volume.
patterns.play_strategy: Find the best blended target window during practice and keep the ball motion smooth through the breakpoint.
patterns.ball_motion: Balanced midlane read with controlled downlane motion.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Start near 11-16 at the arrows and adjust based on the strongest blend of hold and recovery.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Start near 24-29 at the arrows and adjust based on the strongest blend of hold and recovery.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Benchmark reactive or controlled solid with surface matched to lane friction.
patterns.common_adjustments: Make small moves, watch repeated leaves, and change surface or ball shape only after the same reaction appears on quality shots.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Outside zone; short patterns expose this area early, while longer patterns reduce free recovery.
Track and middle blend; match ball speed, surface, and launch angle to this zone.
Mirror-side oil shape for left-side read and cross-lane visual balance.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: Rule-of-31 estimate near board 9, then refine from practice shots.
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Moderate to forward roll to control midlane read and entry angle.
play_profile.friction_response: Balanced response with a narrow but playable blend.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Limited to moderate. Treat inside misses as information, not automatic hold.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Limited. Outside misses can skate if they cross the oil edge too late.
play_profile.hold_area: Small hold area around the selected target window.
play_profile.recovery_area: Recovery is closer to the track than the gutter.
play_profile.speed_control: Repeat speed first; speed misses change breakpoint faster than target moves.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: Higher rev players should smooth response. Lower rev players may need stronger surface to create shape.
play_profile.spare_priority: High priority. Sport conditions reward clean frames and simple spare leaves.

transitions.what_to_watch: Confirm whether the ball reads early, blends, or skids past the breakpoint.
transitions.move_strategy: Start with the official length-based target window and make small moves from repeated shots.
transitions.ball_change: Benchmark reactive or controlled solid.

transitions.what_to_watch: Watch for repeated high hits, weak corners, buckets, or washouts from the same good shot.
transitions.move_strategy: Move with the friction while preserving the breakpoint window.
transitions.ball_change: Change surface or cover only when the current shape no longer controls the pocket.

transitions.what_to_watch: Front-lane friction and carrydown may create over/under.
transitions.move_strategy: Choose the line that leaves the easiest spares and protects the pocket.
transitions.ball_change: Cleaner cover if fronts are burned; more surface if the ball skids through the spot.

equipment_options.when_to_use: Use for the first look at the official pattern before chasing transition.
Use when backend motion is too sharp or pocket control is fragile.
Use when the fronts are gone and the current ball loses continuation.

lane_intelligence.oil_shape: Medium sport pattern with a blended target window.
lane_intelligence.friction_expectation: Friction is present but narrow; repeat launch angle and speed.
lane_intelligence.scoring_pace: Medium scoring with accurate target control.
lane_intelligence.target_window_right: Feet 13-18, eyes 10-16, breakpoint near the rule-of-31 board.
lane_intelligence.target_window_left: Feet 22-27, eyes 24-30, breakpoint near the mirrored rule-of-31 board.
lane_intelligence.breakpoint_window: Start near board 9 from the rule of 31, then refine from practice.
lane_intelligence.miss_risk: Main risk is over-adjusting before the best blend is confirmed.
lane_intelligence.first_move_trigger: Move after repeated good shots show the same leave or breakpoint change.
lane_intelligence.surface_guidance: Start benchmark, then tune surface for backend response.
lane_intelligence.practice_focus: Use practice to confirm breakpoint board, spare reaction, and the first move direction.

external_refs.reference_note: BOWL.com lists Don Johnson as a 40-foot PBA Experience pattern and links the official Kegel PDF with 30.7 mL total oil.
Open Kegel Pattern Library to search this pattern name and verify official graph, load, PDF, and KOSI data before tournament or machine use.
PBA.com lists Don Johnson as a current Legend Pattern oil pattern download. Current PBA versions may differ from legacy BOWL.com/Kegel PDF versions.

patterns.name: Flat Sport Pattern
patterns.summary: A demanding pattern with little side-to-side help from the lane.
patterns.play_strategy: Keep angles controlled and prioritize pocket hits. Accuracy, speed control, and spare shooting matter more than creating a big hook.
patterns.ball_motion: Misses do not self-correct much; shots pushed right tend to stay right and tugged shots can cross high.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Use a direct line near the oil edge for your speed and rev rate, often around 10-13 at the arrows.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Use a direct line near the oil edge for your speed and rev rate, often around 27-30 at the arrows.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Smooth benchmark reactive or urethane depending on friction; avoid overly sharp backend motion early.
patterns.common_adjustments: Make small one-and-one moves. If carry is weak, adjust entry angle before changing balls.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Oil is more even across the lane, so misses receive little help.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: 8-11 board, selected by practice shots rather than free recovery
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Lower to moderate rotation with forward roll; control beats sideways recovery.
play_profile.friction_response: Low recovery and low hold. The lane does not correct direction errors.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Limited. Tugged shots commonly read high or leave splits.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Limited. Pushed shots tend to stay right and miss the pocket.
play_profile.hold_area: Narrow hold near the direct target line, not a true house-shot hold area.
play_profile.recovery_area: Only the natural friction edge; do not count on free hook from the gutter.
play_profile.speed_control: Speed must be within a tight window because speed misses change breakpoint quickly.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: High rev players should tame shape with surface/urethane. Lower rev players need enough surface to create readable hook.
play_profile.spare_priority: High priority. Flat patterns punish open frames more than strike-line misses.

transitions.what_to_watch: Pocket hits are tied directly to target and speed quality.
transitions.move_strategy: Use a direct line and make very small moves.
transitions.ball_change: Smooth benchmark, urethane, or controlled solid based on friction.

transitions.what_to_watch: Shots that were flush start reading high or leaving flat corners.
transitions.move_strategy: Move in small increments and keep the same breakpoint shape.
transitions.ball_change: Change only if the motion window disappears.

transitions.what_to_watch: A playable shim may appear, but misses are still costly.
transitions.move_strategy: Follow the friction gradually; do not chase big swing angles.
transitions.ball_change: Cleaner benchmark if fronts are gone, smoother cover if backend is sharp.

equipment_options.when_to_use: Use when you need readable midlane without a sharp backend move.
Use to keep the pocket in play and reduce sideways miss.
Use when the ball cannot create enough pocket angle.

lane_intelligence.oil_shape: Flat, low-ratio oil with very little built-in side-to-side help.
lane_intelligence.friction_expectation: Friction is narrow and honest; misses usually continue in the direction they were missed.
lane_intelligence.scoring_pace: Lower scoring pace where clean frames beat chasing maximum hook.
lane_intelligence.target_window_right: Feet 12-16, eyes 9-13, breakpoint 8-11 depending on rev rate.
lane_intelligence.target_window_left: Feet 24-28, eyes 27-31, breakpoint 30-33 depending on rev rate.
lane_intelligence.breakpoint_window: Keep breakpoint near the selected oil edge; avoid throwing to the gutter for free recovery.
lane_intelligence.miss_risk: Biggest risk is opening the lane too soon and leaving splits from small misses.
lane_intelligence.first_move_trigger: Move only after multiple quality shots show the same high/weak response.
lane_intelligence.surface_guidance: Smooth 2000-3000 grit benchmark or urethane; avoid sharp covers early.
lane_intelligence.practice_focus: Spend practice finding the safest pocket-control line and spare reaction off the dry.

patterns.name: Bear Style
patterns.summary: A low-forgiveness sport pattern that exposes misses quickly.
patterns.play_strategy: Use a controlled target window and commit to spare shooting. The goal is repeatable pocket control, not maximum hook.
patterns.ball_motion: Minimal built-in hold or recovery; small misses create large result changes.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Use a direct line matched to your ball speed, commonly around 10-14 at the arrows.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Use a direct line matched to your ball speed, commonly around 26-30 at the arrows.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Smooth solid reactive, urethane, or a benchmark ball with surface depending on lane friction.
patterns.common_adjustments: Make smaller moves than on a house shot. Change ball shape when pocket control disappears.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: More hold in the middle, but recovery is less forgiving than a house shot.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: 8-11 board, narrow and accuracy dependent
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Forward roll with minimal shape. Keep the ball stable through the breakpoint.
play_profile.friction_response: Low forgiveness. Both hold and recovery are narrow.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Low. Inside misses can check early or leave difficult splits.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Low. Outside misses rarely recover enough without perfect speed and surface.
play_profile.hold_area: Small hold zone around the target line; do not assume free hold inside.
play_profile.recovery_area: Small recovery window just outside target. Bigger swing creates trouble.
play_profile.speed_control: Speed repeatability is critical. Half-mile-per-hour misses can change the pocket.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: Higher rev players should reduce response. Lower rev players need surface but must avoid early roll-out.
play_profile.spare_priority: Highest priority. Scores survive through clean frames and simple spare systems.

transitions.what_to_watch: Every miss shows immediately. Pocket control is fragile.
transitions.move_strategy: Pick a direct line and repeat speed.
transitions.ball_change: Smooth solid, urethane, or benchmark depending on friction.

transitions.what_to_watch: Two-board misses produce different leaves.
transitions.move_strategy: Make small moves and keep eyes disciplined.
transitions.ball_change: Change to smooth out shape, not to create more hook.

transitions.what_to_watch: Lane shape may still be narrow while spares decide score.
transitions.move_strategy: Play the highest-percentage pocket-control line.
transitions.ball_change: Use whatever gives the simplest spare leaves.

equipment_options.when_to_use: Use when repeatable pocket control is the main goal.
Use to reduce backend violence and avoid splits.
Use when the ball cannot face up enough at the pocket.

lane_intelligence.oil_shape: Flat sport pattern with narrow hold and narrow recovery.
lane_intelligence.friction_expectation: Friction is honest and unforgiving; the lane exposes speed and target misses.
lane_intelligence.scoring_pace: Low scoring pace where spare conversion and simple leaves matter.
lane_intelligence.breakpoint_window: Use the highest-percentage direct breakpoint window; avoid unnecessary swing.
lane_intelligence.miss_risk: Main risk is turning small misses into splits by forcing shape.
lane_intelligence.first_move_trigger: Move after repeated high-quality shots show the same miss, not after one bad shot.
lane_intelligence.surface_guidance: Smooth solid, low-response reactive, or urethane at 1000-3000 grit.
lane_intelligence.practice_focus: Find the line that leaves the easiest spares when the pocket is missed.

patterns.name: Blended Challenge 42
patterns.summary: A bridge pattern between house and sport conditions with some hold in the middle and less free recovery outside.
patterns.play_strategy: Use the track area first, then decide whether the outside friction or the middle hold gives the best carry. Miss room exists, but it is narrower than a house shot.
patterns.ball_motion: Readable midlane motion with a controlled backend move when the breakpoint is repeated.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Start around 13-16 at the arrows with a breakpoint near 8-11.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Start around 25-28 at the arrows with a breakpoint near 30-33.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Benchmark reactive or smooth hybrid with moderate surface.
patterns.common_adjustments: Move in small pairs as the track develops. If over/under appears, smooth the ball motion before chasing deeper angles.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Outside boards have some recovery, but less than a house pattern.
Track zone blends hold and recovery for controlled angles.
Middle oil provides hold without creating a full house-shot wall.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: 9-12 board with controllable recovery
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Moderate rotation with a stable roll. Too much side rotation can create over/under as the track opens.
play_profile.friction_response: Balanced response. The lane gives some recovery but still requires repeatable projection.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Moderate. Middle hold exists, but tugged shots can still check high once friction develops.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Moderate. Outside misses can recover if they see friction before the end of the pattern.
play_profile.hold_area: Boards 18-28 provide usable hold without a full house-shot wall.
play_profile.recovery_area: Track friction around 7-11 is the first recovery zone.
play_profile.speed_control: Keep speed repeatable and make smaller moves than on a house shot.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: Lower rev players can start with stronger cover. Higher rev players should smooth response.
play_profile.spare_priority: Medium-high priority. This pattern is scorable, but opens appear when angle misses grow.

external_refs.reference_note: Open Kegel Pattern Library to search this pattern name and verify official graph, load, PDF, and KOSI data before tournament or machine use.

patterns.name: Mark Roth Style
patterns.summary: An official BOWL.com PBA Experience hall of fame pattern with verified PDF length and volume.
patterns.play_strategy: Find the best blended target window during practice and keep the ball motion smooth through the breakpoint.
patterns.ball_motion: Balanced midlane read with controlled downlane motion.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Start near 11-16 at the arrows and adjust based on the strongest blend of hold and recovery.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Start near 24-29 at the arrows and adjust based on the strongest blend of hold and recovery.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Benchmark reactive or controlled solid with surface matched to lane friction.
patterns.common_adjustments: Make small moves, watch repeated leaves, and change surface or ball shape only after the same reaction appears on quality shots.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Outside zone; short patterns expose this area early, while longer patterns reduce free recovery.
Track and middle blend; match ball speed, surface, and launch angle to this zone.
Mirror-side oil shape for left-side read and cross-lane visual balance.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: Rule-of-31 estimate near board 11, then refine from practice shots.
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Moderate to forward roll to control midlane read and entry angle.
play_profile.friction_response: Balanced response with a narrow but playable blend.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Limited to moderate. Treat inside misses as information, not automatic hold.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Limited. Outside misses can skate if they cross the oil edge too late.
play_profile.hold_area: Small hold area around the selected target window.
play_profile.recovery_area: Recovery is closer to the track than the gutter.
play_profile.speed_control: Repeat speed first; speed misses change breakpoint faster than target moves.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: Higher rev players should smooth response. Lower rev players may need stronger surface to create shape.
play_profile.spare_priority: High priority. Sport conditions reward clean frames and simple spare leaves.

transitions.what_to_watch: Confirm whether the ball reads early, blends, or skids past the breakpoint.
transitions.move_strategy: Start with the official length-based target window and make small moves from repeated shots.
transitions.ball_change: Benchmark reactive or controlled solid.

transitions.what_to_watch: Watch for repeated high hits, weak corners, buckets, or washouts from the same good shot.
transitions.move_strategy: Move with the friction while preserving the breakpoint window.
transitions.ball_change: Change surface or cover only when the current shape no longer controls the pocket.

transitions.what_to_watch: Front-lane friction and carrydown may create over/under.
transitions.move_strategy: Choose the line that leaves the easiest spares and protects the pocket.
transitions.ball_change: Cleaner cover if fronts are burned; more surface if the ball skids through the spot.

equipment_options.when_to_use: Use for the first look at the official pattern before chasing transition.
Use when backend motion is too sharp or pocket control is fragile.
Use when the fronts are gone and the current ball loses continuation.

lane_intelligence.oil_shape: Medium sport pattern with a blended target window.
lane_intelligence.friction_expectation: Friction is present but narrow; repeat launch angle and speed.
lane_intelligence.scoring_pace: Medium scoring with accurate target control.
lane_intelligence.target_window_right: Feet 13-18, eyes 10-16, breakpoint near the rule-of-31 board.
lane_intelligence.target_window_left: Feet 22-27, eyes 24-30, breakpoint near the mirrored rule-of-31 board.
lane_intelligence.breakpoint_window: Start near board 11 from the rule of 31, then refine from practice.
lane_intelligence.miss_risk: Main risk is over-adjusting before the best blend is confirmed.
lane_intelligence.first_move_trigger: Move after repeated good shots show the same leave or breakpoint change.
lane_intelligence.surface_guidance: Start benchmark, then tune surface for backend response.
lane_intelligence.practice_focus: Use practice to confirm breakpoint board, spare reaction, and the first move direction.

patterns.name: Scorpion Style
patterns.summary: A medium-long pattern that rewards controlled ball motion and good launch angles.
patterns.play_strategy: Avoid giving away the pocket with too much side rotation. Blend the wet/dry and keep the ball in front of you until transition is clear.
patterns.ball_motion: Skids longer than medium house conditions and needs a controlled move downlane.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Start near 14-17 at the arrows with a breakpoint around 8-10.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Start near 23-26 at the arrows with a breakpoint around 31-33.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Solid reactive or hybrid reactive with moderate surface for control.
patterns.common_adjustments: If the ball labors, move right or use more surface. If it jumps late, move in and smooth the shape.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: More hold in the middle, but recovery is less forgiving than a house shot.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: 9-12 board, with controlled projection
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Forward-rolling release with moderate axis rotation; avoid excessive side rotation.
play_profile.friction_response: Length creates delayed response. The ball must read the midlane without burning up.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Small to moderate. Tugged shots hold briefly but can drive high late.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Small. Outside misses often lack recovery if they cross the oil edge too late.
play_profile.hold_area: Hold sits around the middle-track blend; deeper misses can skate.
play_profile.recovery_area: Recovery is around the outside of the track, not the gutter.
play_profile.speed_control: Keep speed from floating high. Too much speed makes the ball miss the spot downlane.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: Higher rev players can open angles once friction develops. Lower rev players need surface early.
play_profile.spare_priority: High priority because pocket control can leave makeable spares if misses stay small.

transitions.what_to_watch: Ball skids farther and may leave weak corners if it misses midlane.
transitions.move_strategy: Use surface and keep the launch angle in front of you.
transitions.ball_change: Solid or hybrid with readable midlane.

transitions.what_to_watch: Ball starts reading sooner in the track.
transitions.move_strategy: Move in gradually and preserve downlane angle.
transitions.ball_change: Cleaner cover if early roll appears.

transitions.what_to_watch: Shots jump from friction or skate through carrydown.
transitions.move_strategy: Choose control or angle based on carry, not just pocket hits.
transitions.ball_change: Ball up for carrydown; ball down for early hook.

equipment_options.when_to_use: Use to make the ball see the midlane on fresh medium-long oil.
Use when solid covers read too early but pearl is too sharp.
Use when the fronts are burned and you still need continuation.

lane_intelligence.oil_shape: Medium-long pattern with more hold inside and delayed downlane response.
lane_intelligence.friction_expectation: Ball needs to read the midlane; too-clean equipment can skate past the breakpoint.
lane_intelligence.scoring_pace: Medium scoring with premium on entry angle control.
lane_intelligence.breakpoint_window: Breakpoint is usually outside the track but not gutter-adjacent.
lane_intelligence.miss_risk: Main risk is weak corners from skid or high splits when the ball sees friction late.
lane_intelligence.first_move_trigger: Move when the ball starts reading the track earlier or repeated flat corners appear.
lane_intelligence.surface_guidance: Solid or controlled hybrid at 2000-3000 grit for a readable midlane.
lane_intelligence.practice_focus: Use practice to test whether stronger surface creates continuation or roll-out.

patterns.name: Carmen Salvino Style
patterns.summary: An official BOWL.com PBA Experience hall of fame pattern with verified PDF length and volume.
patterns.play_strategy: Keep the ball in the oil longer, control speed, and avoid giving away the pocket with too much early angle.
patterns.ball_motion: Longer skid phase with later, more controlled hook.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Start deeper or more direct, often around 16-22 at the arrows, keeping the breakpoint inside the dry edge.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Start deeper or more direct from the left side, often around 18-24 at the arrows, keeping the breakpoint inside the dry edge.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Stronger solid or hybrid reactive with enough surface to read the midlane.
patterns.common_adjustments: Make small moves, watch repeated leaves, and change surface or ball shape only after the same reaction appears on quality shots.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Outside zone; short patterns expose this area early, while longer patterns reduce free recovery.
Track and middle blend; match ball speed, surface, and launch angle to this zone.
Mirror-side oil shape for left-side read and cross-lane visual balance.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: Rule-of-31 estimate near board 13, then refine from practice shots.
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Moderate to forward roll to control midlane read and entry angle.
play_profile.friction_response: Delayed response; the ball must read before the breakpoint.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Limited to moderate. Treat inside misses as information, not automatic hold.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Limited. Outside misses can skate if they cross the oil edge too late.
play_profile.hold_area: Small hold area around the selected target window.
play_profile.recovery_area: Recovery is closer to the track than the gutter.
play_profile.speed_control: Repeat speed first; speed misses change breakpoint faster than target moves.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: Higher rev players should smooth response. Lower rev players may need stronger surface to create shape.
play_profile.spare_priority: High priority. Sport conditions reward clean frames and simple spare leaves.

transitions.what_to_watch: Confirm whether the ball reads early, blends, or skids past the breakpoint.
transitions.move_strategy: Start with the official length-based target window and make small moves from repeated shots.
transitions.ball_change: Stronger solid or hybrid with surface.

transitions.what_to_watch: Watch for repeated high hits, weak corners, buckets, or washouts from the same good shot.
transitions.move_strategy: Move with the friction while preserving the breakpoint window.
transitions.ball_change: Change surface or cover only when the current shape no longer controls the pocket.

transitions.what_to_watch: Front-lane friction and carrydown may create over/under.
transitions.move_strategy: Choose the line that leaves the easiest spares and protects the pocket.
transitions.ball_change: Cleaner cover if fronts are burned; more surface if the ball skids through the spot.

equipment_options.when_to_use: Use for the first look at the official pattern before chasing transition.
Use when backend motion is too sharp or pocket control is fragile.
Use when the fronts are gone and the current ball loses continuation.

lane_intelligence.oil_shape: Longer sport pattern that pushes breakpoint deeper.
lane_intelligence.friction_expectation: Friction is delayed; the ball must pick up before it reaches the end of the pattern.
lane_intelligence.scoring_pace: Lower to medium scoring unless ball motion matches volume.
lane_intelligence.target_window_right: Feet 18-26, eyes 16-22, breakpoint inside the dry edge.
lane_intelligence.target_window_left: Feet 14-22, eyes 18-24, breakpoint inside the dry edge.
lane_intelligence.breakpoint_window: Start near board 13 from the rule of 31, then refine from practice.
lane_intelligence.miss_risk: Main risk is washouts or weak corners from skidding through the breakpoint.
lane_intelligence.first_move_trigger: Move after repeated good shots show the same leave or breakpoint change.
lane_intelligence.surface_guidance: Use enough surface to read the midlane without burning up.
lane_intelligence.practice_focus: Use practice to confirm breakpoint board, spare reaction, and the first move direction.

patterns.name: Shark Style
patterns.summary: A long pattern where the ball hooks later and outside misses have less recovery.
patterns.play_strategy: Keep the ball in the oil longer and avoid early outside angles. Create pocket control with straighter launch and forward roll.
patterns.ball_motion: Long skid with late, controlled hook when matched correctly.
patterns.suggested_line_right: Play inside or direct through the track, often 18-22 at the arrows with a breakpoint near 10-13.
patterns.suggested_line_left: Play inside or direct from the left side, often 18-22 boards mirrored from the left with a breakpoint near 28-31.
patterns.recommended_equipment: Strong solid reactive with surface for most players; straighter players may need less surface to retain energy.
patterns.common_adjustments: If the ball never corners, add surface or move right. If it reads too early, reduce surface or move deeper.
patterns.source_note: Use as an app reference. Verify official lane graphs before tournament play.

zones.note: Long oil keeps the ball in skid longer and reduces outside recovery.

play_profile.breakpoint_range: 13-17 board, usually deeper and straighter
play_profile.ideal_axis_rotation: Forward roll, lower axis rotation, and controlled tilt to keep the ball from skidding past the breakpoint.
play_profile.friction_response: Late response with little outside recovery. Shape must be created through oil, not dry boards.
play_profile.inside_miss_room: Moderate hold inside, but shots can sail if the ball never reads.
play_profile.outside_miss_room: Low. Outside misses are the danger because long oil delays recovery.
play_profile.hold_area: The oil volume inside helps hold, but too much hold becomes flat corners or washouts.
play_profile.recovery_area: Recovery is deeper than most patterns, often near 12-15 rather than the gutter.
play_profile.speed_control: Avoid throwing through the breakpoint. Slight speed reduction or more surface may be needed.
play_profile.rev_rate_matchup: High rev players can blend the pattern from deeper. Lower rev players usually need stronger solid covers.
play_profile.spare_priority: Very high priority. Long patterns create buckets, washouts, and weak corners when speed/shape misses.

transitions.what_to_watch: Ball delays hook and outside misses do not recover.
transitions.move_strategy: Play deeper/direct and make the ball read before the breakpoint.
transitions.ball_change: Strong solid with surface for most bowlers.

transitions.what_to_watch: The ball finally starts picking up in the midlane.
transitions.move_strategy: Move with the oil line and keep breakpoint inside the dry.
transitions.ball_change: Stay strong if corners are weak; cleaner only if fronts are gone.

transitions.what_to_watch: One shot checks early, the next skids too far.
transitions.move_strategy: Reduce shape and control speed; avoid chasing the gutter.
transitions.ball_change: Surface adjustment can be more useful than a large ball change.

equipment_options.when_to_use: Use to create midlane traction and avoid skidding through the breakpoint.
Use to blend the long oil without burning up too early.
Use when the heads hook but carry still requires a strong shape.

lane_intelligence.oil_shape: Long oil pattern that pushes breakpoint deeper and limits outside recovery.
lane_intelligence.friction_expectation: Outside friction is delayed; shots leaked too far right often stay right.
lane_intelligence.scoring_pace: Lower to medium scoring unless the ball matches volume and entry angle.
lane_intelligence.breakpoint_window: Keep breakpoint inside the dry edge; long oil usually rewards deeper control.
lane_intelligence.miss_risk: Main risk is washouts and weak corners from throwing through the breakpoint.
lane_intelligence.first_move_trigger: Move or add surface when good shots cannot face up or corners are consistently flat.
lane_intelligence.surface_guidance: Strong solid at 1000-2000 grit early; reduce surface only when fronts disappear.
lane_intelligence.practice_focus: Confirm the earliest board where the ball reads without burning up.
